findings.title: Dead acknowledgedRef variable never set to true
findings.why_it_matters: The acknowledgedRef ref was always false, making its guard condition in the accept callback dead code. This was likely an unfinished feature meant to prevent double-accept but was never wired to be set to true.
evidence: acknowledgedRef is declared at line 97 with useRef(false), set to false at line 110 in useEffect, but never set to true. The guard `if (!suggestion || acknowledgedRef.current) return` at line 84 never activates from acknowledgedRef. acceptedRef already handles double-accept prevention (set to true at line 85).
findings.suggested_fix: Remove acknowledgedRef and its references. The acceptedRef and suggestion null-check already prevent double-accept.

findings.title: Removed silent-catch-ok comment triggers ESLint warning
findings.why_it_matters: The original code had a // silent-catch-ok comment justifying the silent catch on loadImportedCourses(). The comment was accidentally removed during refactoring, triggering a project ESLint rule violation.
evidence: Original code at diff line 1521 had comment: '// silent-catch-ok: store refresh failure is non-critical; courses are already in state'. New code at line 150 has no comment. ESLint error-handling/no-silent-catch triggered.
findings.suggested_fix: Restore the // silent-catch-ok comment before the .catch() call.

findings.title: Missing silent-catch-ok comment on background computation catch
findings.why_it_matters: The compute() catch block silently handles failures, which is correct for a background computation. But it lacks the project-required // silent-catch-ok annotation.
evidence: Catch at line 130 has console.warn but no // silent-catch-ok comment. ESLint error-handling/no-silent-catch warning triggered.
findings.suggested_fix: Add // silent-catch-ok: background preference computation failure is non-critical comment.

residual_risks: The accept callback references acknowledgedRef which was declared later in the file -- while not a runtime bug (closure semantics), the ordering violates React hook conventions
testing_gaps: No test verifying useAutoPlacement accept callback prevents double-accept
No test verifying useAutoPlacement dismiss callback prevents stale suggestions
